letterSpacing.$description: Primitive letter-spacing values for the River Romney design system. Negative values tighten tracking; positive values open it. Do not reference these tokens directly in UI code; use semantic tokens when available.

tight.$description: Slightly reduced tracking. Used for large display text where modest tracking reduction improves visual weight. Referenced by: letterSpacing.display.

tightest.$description: Most reduced tracking in the system. Used for headings to tighten letter-spacing at medium display sizes. Referenced by: letterSpacing.title.

normal.$description: Default tracking. No adjustment to the typeface's built-in letter-spacing. The baseline for all body text and most UI text. Referenced by: letterSpacing.body.

subtle.$description: Barely-open tracking — a third of `wide`. Used by the decision-engine label override: Geist at data-UI density reads better with minimal tracking than the base 0.03em. Referenced by: decision-engine letterSpacing.label.

wide.$description: Slightly open tracking. Used for UI labels and navigation where a small amount of open tracking improves readability at small sizes. Referenced by: letterSpacing.label.

wider.$description: Generously open tracking. For all-caps labels and display contexts where wide tracking is a deliberate typographic choice. Do not use at body sizes — wide tracking at 16px or below reads as broken text. Referenced by: letterSpacing.all-caps.
